Let me create a comprehensive report:\n\n## COMPREHENSIVE SINGULARITY SKILL REPORT\n\n---\n\n## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY\n\nSingularity is a consulting engagement orchestrator that transforms raw meeting transcripts, call preps, and emails into structured research libraries, client-facing deliverables, and pricing models. It replaces and supersedes the older sales-forge skill. The skill uses a \"blockchain methodology\"—immutable, chronologically-numbered research documents that preserve the discovery arc—combined with parallel agent architecture for transcript decomposition and multi-pass reading for exhaustive information capture.\n\n---\n\n## PURPOSE AND SCOPE\n\n**What Singularity Does:**\n- Organizes consulting engagements from raw source material into professionally structured folder hierarchies\n- Decomposes meeting transcripts and documents through multi-pass, focused reading\n- Maintains an immutable research library (blockchain-style) that records the complete discovery arc\n- Spawns parallel agents to extract topical deep dives from transcripts simultaneously\n- Tracks stakeholders and maintains an always-current org chart\n- Drafts client-facing deliverables (problem restatements, information requests, preliminary approaches, formal proposals)\n- Supports pricing questionnaire workflows and Excel workbook generation\n- Facilitates strategic discussions with Claude to refine engagement positioning\n\n**When to Use Singularity:**\n- New client opportunity or engagement\n- Transcript/document processing\n- Engagement preparation and discovery\n- Proposal creation and deliverable generation\n- Pricing model development and iteration\n- Strategic thinking through engagement challenges\n\n**When NOT to Use:**\n- Internal project work (use other tools)\n- Code implementation tasks\n- Non-consulting work\n\n---\n\n## HARD RULES (MANDATORY)\n\n1. **Blockchain Style**: Research documents are numbered chronologically and NEVER edited after creation. New understanding goes into new numbered documents that reference earlier ones.\n\n2. **Multi-Pass Processing**: Transcripts are read multiple times, each pass focused on one topic. This is foundational for context clearing, exhaustive capture, better organization, and natural parallelization. Write after every read, not once at the end.\n\n3. **Ask, Do Not Assume**: Always propose file lists and get user approval before writing research docs or spawning agents. The user controls what gets created.\n\n4. **Agents Write Their Own Files**: Deep-dive and research agents write directly to the engagement folder using `mode: \"bypassPermissions\"`. Never fall back to writing in the main session if an agent fails.\n\n5. **Max 5 Questions Per Batch**: Never overwhelm with more than 5 follow-up questions at a time. Ask in subsequent rounds and prioritize questions that gate decisions.\n\n6. **Deliverables Flow from Research**: Client-facing documents are drafted from the research library, not from raw transcripts. The research library is the single source of truth.\n\n7. **Everything is Dated**: Every file in the engagement gets a date in its filename (YYYY-MM-DD). No exceptions. Date reflects the source material date, not the analysis date.\n\n8. **No Em Dashes in Deliverables**: Use commas, periods, \"and,\" or parentheses instead. Also: no individual names, no direct quotes, no contrastive framing (\"It's not just X, it's Y\"), no emojis, no contractions.\n\n9. **Solution, Never Product**: BayOne builds custom, tailor-made solutions using proven methodology. Never position as an off-the-shelf product.\n\n10. **Self-Contained Skill**: All reference files, templates, and assets live within `.claude/skills/singularity/`. No external dependencies except `.claude/context/` for shared company info.\n\n---\n\n## FOLDER STRUCTURE: COMPLETE SPECIFICATION\n\n### Engagement Root\nEvery engagement lives at `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/` at the project root (not in `.claude/`).\n\n**Example paths:**\n```\n/lam_research/ip_protection/\n/cisco/cicd_implementation/\n/acme_corp/platform_modernization/\n```\n\n### Full Engagement Folder Tree\n\n```\n/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/\n│\n├── org_chart.md\n│   └─ Living document, always current. Tracks all stakeholders and relationship dynamics.\n│\n├── source/                          [Raw input files. NEVER modified.]\n│   ├── call_prep_2026-03-12.txt\n│   ├── discovery_call_transcript_2026-03-12.txt\n│   ├── internal_debrief_2026-03-12.txt\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── research/                        [Blockchain decomposition. Append-only.]\n│   ├── 00_methodology_<date>.md          (First file, explains the system)\n│   ├── 01_*_<date>.md                    (First document set)\n│   ├── 02_*_<date>.md                    (Second document set)\n│   ├── 02a_*_<date>.md                   (Supplementary to set 02)\n│   ├── 01-02_changes_<date>.md           (Bridge document)\n│   ├── 03_*_<date>.md                    (Third document set)\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── planning/                        [Approach planning, notes, handoffs. Editable.]\n│   ├── skill_notes.md                    (Accumulated do's/don'ts for this engagement)\n│   ├── glossary_<date>.md                (Optional: key terms and definitions)\n│   ├── session_handoff_<date>.md         (Where last session left off)\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── pricing/                         [Pricing specs, corrections, workbooks. Versioned.]\n│   ├── pricing_spec_<date>.md            (Output of questionnaire, prompt for Excel)\n│   ├── pricing_corrections_v1_<date>.md  (Iterative feedback for Excel)\n│   ├── pricing_corrections_v2_<date>.md\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── deliverables/                    [Client-facing documents. Flat, dated.]\n│   ├── problem_restatement_<date>.md\n│   ├── problem_restatement_<date>.html\n│   ├── information_request_<date>.md\n│   ├── information_request_<date>.html\n│   ├── preliminary_approach_<date>.md\n│   ├── preliminary_approach_<date>.html\n│   ├── formal_proposal_<date>.md\n│   ├── formal_proposal_<date>.html\n│   ├── followup_email_<date>.md\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── presentations/                   [Slide decks, presentation materials. Dated.]\n│   ├── discovery_findings_<date>.html\n│   ├── technical_approach_<date>.html\n│   ├── executive_briefing_<date>.html\n│   └── ...\n│\n├── decisions/                       [Open questions and agreed decisions. Editable.]\n│   └── ...\n│\n└── progress/                        [Running status tracking. Editable.]\n    └── ...\n```\n\n### Folder Purposes Summary\n\n| Folder | Purpose | Mutability | Dated? |\n|--------|---------|------------|--------|\n| `source/` | Raw input files (transcripts, emails, docs) | Never modified | Yes (source date) |\n| `research/` | Blockchain decomposition documents | Append-only (new files only, never edit old) | Yes (source date) |\n| `planning/` | Approach planning, skill notes, glossary, session handoffs | Editable | Yes |\n| `pricing/` | Pricing specs, correction prompts, cost models, workbooks | Editable, versioned | Yes |\n| `deliverables/` | Client-facing documents (markdown + HTML) | Editable, versioned | Yes |\n| `presentations/` | Slide decks, pitch materials, executive briefings | Editable, versioned | Yes |\n| `decisions/` | Open questions and agreed decisions | Editable | Yes |\n| `progress/` | Running status tracking | Editable | Yes |\n\n---\n\n## FILE NAMING CONVENTIONS\n\n### Research Files\n\n**Pattern:** `<set_number>_<source_type>_<topic>_<date>.md`\n\n**Examples:**\n```\n00_methodology_2026-03-20.md\n01_call_prep_situational_context_2026-03-12.md\n01_call_prep_discovery_strategy_2026-03-12.md\n01_call_prep_people_2026-03-12.md\n01_call_prep_summary_2026-03-12.md\n02_meeting_people_2026-03-12.md\n02_meeting_topic_map_2026-03-12.md\n02_meeting_technical_use_cases_2026-03-12.md\n02_meeting_what_was_tried_2026-03-12.md\n02_meeting_summary_2026-03-12.md\n02a_debrief_people_2026-03-12.md\n02a_debrief_internal_assessment_2026-03-12.md\n02a_debrief_summary_2026-03-12.md\n03_discussion_technical_approach_2026-03-20.md\n03_discussion_summary_2026-03-20.md\n```\n\n### Naming Rules\n\n- All lowercase with underscores (snake_case)\n- Set number prefix always two digits (01, 02, 03... not 1, 2, 3)\n- Supplementary sets use letter suffix (02a, 02b, 02c)\n- Date suffix uses ISO format (YYYY-MM-DD)\n- Date reflects the source material date, not the analysis date\n- Descriptive name should be clear enough to understand purpose without opening the file\n\n### Bridge Documents\n\n**Pattern:** `<set_from>-<set_to>_changes_<date>.md`\n\n**Examples:**\n```\n01-02_changes_2026-03-12.md\n02-03_changes_2026-03-20.md\n```\n\nBridge documents capture what changed between two sets: hypotheses validated/invalidated, what was correct/incorrect, new information, questions answered.\n\n### Standard Files Per Set\n\n| File Type | Pattern | When Created |\n|-----------|---------|--------------|\n| Methodology | `00_methodology_<date>.md` | Once, at engagement start |\n| People | `<set>_<source_type>_people_<date>.md` | First file for transcript sets |\n| Topic map | `<set>_<source_type>_topic_map_<date>.md` | After first pass of transcript |\n| Summary | `<set>_<source_type>_summary_<date>.md` | Last file for every set |\n| Bridge | `<from>-<to>_changes_<date>.md` | After both sets exist |\n\n### Deliverable Files\n\nDeliverables live in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/deliverables/` as flat, dated files:\n\n```\nproblem_restatement_2026-03-20.md\nproblem_restatement_2026-03-20.html\ninformation_request_2026-03-20.md\ninformation_request_2026-03-20.html\npreliminary_approach_2026-03-20.md\npreliminary_approach_2026-03-20.html\nformal_proposal_2026-04-15.md\nformal_proposal_2026-04-15.html\nfollowup_email_2026-03-20.md\n```\n\nPresentations go in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/presentations/` as flat, dated files:\n\n```\ndiscovery_findings_2026-03-20.html\ntechnical_approach_2026-04-01.html\nexecutive_briefing_2026-04-15.html\n```\n\n### Document Header Format\n\nEvery research document must start with this header (MANDATORY):\n\n```markdown\n# <Set Number> - <Source Type>: <Topic>\n\n**Source:** /<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/source/<filename>\n**Source Date:** <date> (<context description>)\n**Document Set:** <set number> (<description of what this set covers>)\n**Pass:** <what this specific read was focused on>\n\n---\n```\n\n**Example:**\n```markdown\n# 02 - Meeting: Technical Use Cases (Deep Dive)\n\n**Source:** /lam_research/ip_protection/source/lam_meeting_3122026.txt\n**Source Date:** 2026-03-12 (Discovery Call)\n**Document Set:** 02 (Meeting Transcript)\n**Pass:** Focused deep dive on use cases\n\n---\n```\n\n---\n\n## BLOCKCHAIN METHODOLOGY: CORE CONCEPT\n\n### What It Means\n\nAll research documents are numbered chronologically and are append-only. Once a document is written, it is NEVER edited or revised. New understanding, corrections, or evolving thinking gets captured in subsequent documents that reference back to earlier ones.\n\nThis is called \"blockchain style\" because, like a blockchain, each entry is immutable and the chain is read forward to reconstruct the full state.\n\n### Why It Exists\n\n1. **Chronological Progression of Thought**: Consulting engagements span multiple meetings, multiple Claude sessions, and evolving understanding. Preserving each document as written maintains a clear record of what was known at each point in time, not a retroactively cleaned-up version.\n\n2. **No Re-Explanation Tax**: A new Claude session or team member can read forward through numbered documents and reconstruct the full arc of discovery, including where assumptions were wrong and when corrections happened. No re-explanation needed.\n\n3. **Order of Operations Clarity**: When information arrives from different meetings, emails, and conversations, the numbering system makes it unambiguous which information was available at which point and what informed what.\n\n4. **Honest Record**: Early hypotheses that turn out to be wrong are just as valuable as correct ones. They show what was validated, what was invalidated, and why thinking evolved. This prevents revisionist history and supports better decision-making.\n\n### Numbering Convention\n\n**Document Set Number:** The prefix number (01, 02, 03...) represents a chronological document set. All files from the same source event share a prefix.\n\n- `01_*` = first source event processed\n- `02_*` = second source event\n- `03_*` = third source event\n\n**There is nothing special about what number a set gets.** 01 is not always \"call prep,\" 02 is not always \"a meeting,\" 03 is not always \"a discussion.\" The number reflects when in the project timeline the material was created or processed.\n\n**Supplementary Material (Letter Suffixes):** When supplementary material is tied to the same event as an existing document set (e.g., an internal debrief immediately after a meeting, a follow-up email the same day), use a letter suffix rather than a new number:\n\n- `02_meeting_*` = the meeting itself\n- `02a_debrief_*` = internal debrief after the same meeting\n- `02b_followup_email_*` = a follow-up email about the same meeting\n\nThis keeps it clear that supplementary material is event-adjacent, not a new chronological step.\n\n### Standard Files Per Set\n\n**Required for Every Set:**\n1. **Summary document** (e.g., `01_call_prep_summary_2026-03-12.md`)\n   - Always the LAST file written for each set\n   - Short, high-level overview of everything covered in the set\n   - References the other files by name\n   - Lets a new Claude session understand the set without reading every detail file\n   - Critical because the number and type of detail files varies per set\n\n**Required for Sets Based on Meetings/Calls:**\n2. **People document** (e.g., `02_meeting_people_2026-03-12.md`)\n   - Who was on the call, roles, titles, sentiment\n   - Always created for any transcript-based set\n\n**Created at the End (After Multiple Sets Exist):**\n3. **Bridge documents** (e.g., `01-02_changes_2026-03-12.md`)\n   - Captures what changed between two document sets\n   - What was confirmed, invalidated, or newly learned\n   - Written retrospectively after both sets exist\n   - Never written during processing\n\n### Rules\n\n1. Never go back and edit a numbered document after it is written.\n2. New insights go in new documents with higher numbers.\n3. Reference back to earlier documents by number when correcting or building on them.\n4. Each document set covers one source event (a meeting, an email, a document, a discussion).\n5. The methodology document (`00_methodology_<date>.md`) is the ONLY file that can be updated because it describes the system itself.\n6. The org chart is the ONLY other file that can be updated (see People Tracking section).\n\n### Reading Order for a New Session\n\nA new Claude session picking up an engagement should read in this order:\n\n1. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/00_methodology_<date>.md` to understand the system\n2. Summary documents in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/` in order (01 summary, 02 summary, 02a summary, etc.)\n3. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` for current people state\n4. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/skill_notes.md` if it exists (accumulated do's/don'ts)\n5. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/session_handoff_<date>.md` if it exists (where last session left off)\n6. Then dive into specific detail files as needed\n\n---\n\n## WORKFLOW PHASES\n\nSingularity has 6 main workflows plus a mandatory startup check:\n\n### STARTUP: Permission Check (EVERY INVOCATION)\n\nBefore ANY work, the skill MUST:\n\n1. Read `.claude/settings.local.json`\n2. Verify `Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/**)` exists in `permissions.allow`\n3. Verify `WebSearch` exists in `permissions.allow`\n4. If either is missing:\n   - Tell the user: \"Singularity requires agents to write files autonomously and research agents to search the web. I need to add `Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/**)` and/or `WebSearch` to the permissions. May I do that?\"\n   - If approved, add the permission\n   - If declined, explain the skill cannot function and stop\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 1: NEW ENGAGEMENT\n\n**Questions to ask:**\n1. What is the client name? (lowercase, underscores for folder name)\n2. What is the opportunity name? (lowercase, underscores)\n3. What source material do you have? (transcripts, emails, call preps, documents)\n4. Do you want the full folder structure or a subset? (Default: all. Minimum: source + research.)\n\n**Actions:**\n1. Create `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/` with selected folders\n2. Write `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/00_methodology_<date>.md` using the template at `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/templates/methodology_template.md`. Fill in the client name and opportunity description.\n3. Read `.claude/context/bayone_team.md` for BayOne team context\n4. Begin processing source material (proceed to Flow 3)\n\n**Load references:**\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/folder_structure.md`\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/blockchain_methodology.md`\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 2: CONTINUE ENGAGEMENT\n\n**Questions to ask:**\n1. Which engagement? (client/opportunity, or detect from context)\n\n**Actions (read in this order):**\n1. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/session_handoff_<date>.md` if exists (where last session left off)\n2. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/00_methodology_<date>.md` (the system)\n3. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/skill_notes.md` if exists (accumulated do's/don'ts)\n4. Summary docs in order from `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/` (01 summary, 02 summary, 02a summary, etc.)\n5. `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` (current people state)\n6. Report current state and ask what to do next\n\n**Load references:**\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/session_continuity.md`\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 3: PROCESS SOURCE MATERIAL\n\n**Questions to ask:**\n1. Which engagement? (if not already established)\n2. What type of source? (transcript, call prep, email, debrief, other)\n3. Is this a new event (new set number) or supplementary to a prior event (letter suffix like 02a)?\n\n**Load references:**\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/document_processing.md`\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/agent_architecture.md`\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/people_tracking.md`\n\n#### Transcripts (Primary Use Case)\n\n**Exact processing order:**\n\n1. **Read prior context.** Latest summary in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/` + `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md`. This ensures continuity and avoids re-discovery.\n\n2. **Pass 1: People file.** Read the full transcript. Write the people document to `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_<type>_people_<date>.md`. Always the first file for transcript sets.\n\n3. **Pass 1 continued: Topic map.** Identify major topics. Write topic map with proposed deep-dive files AND rationale for why each needs its own file. Present to user.\n\n4. **Ask user.** Get approval on the file list. User may adjust, add, or remove files.\n\n5. **Spawn parallel agents.** One agent per topic, ALL spawned in a single message for maximum parallelism. Each agent reads the full transcript with ONE topic focus and writes one file. See Agent Prompt Template below.\n\n6. **Update org chart.** After all agents complete, update `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` with everything learned.\n\n7. **Bridge document.** If this is not the first set, write `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<from>-<to>_changes_<date>.md`. See `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/bridge_document_example.md` for the gold standard format. Captures: hypotheses validated/invalidated/open, what we got wrong, what we got right, new information, questions answered.\n\n8. **Summary document.** Always last. Short overview referencing all files in the set.\n\n9. **Offer research.** If unfamiliar technologies or terms were encountered, offer to spawn research agents.\n\n#### Agent Prompt Template for Transcript Deep Dives\n\nEvery deep-dive agent must receive this complete, self-contained prompt:\n\n```\nYou are writing a detailed decomposition document from a meeting transcript.\nRead the transcript, then write one focused output file.\n\n**Your focus:** [ONE SPECIFIC TOPIC - e.g., \"Technical use cases and requirements\"]\n\n**Read this file:** /<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/source/<transcript_filename>\n\n**Context from the topic map (so you know what to look for):**\n[BULLET POINTS from the topic map for this specific topic]\n\n**What to capture in exhaustive detail:**\n- Quote or closely paraphrase key statements. Do not summarize. Decompose.\n- Capture specific numbers, names, technical details, and claims.\n- Note who said what when it matters for context.\n- Flag open questions or unresolved points.\n\n**IMPORTANT: Speech-to-text transcript quality.**\nThis transcript is speech-to-text and full of transcription errors. Use common sense:\n- \"concept-renade\" = \"confidential violation\"\n- \"lamb GPT\" = \"LamGPT\"\n- \"Spacey\" = \"SpaCy\"\n- \"on pram\" = \"on-prem\"\n- \"brass beam\" = \"Brad's team\"\n- \"compiler\" = \"Copilot\"\n- \"Gapsium\" / \"Kaptima\" = \"Capgemini\"\nInfer intended meaning from context. Do not take mangled words at face value.\n\n**Write your output to:** /<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_<type>_<topic>_<date>.md\n\n**Use this header format:**\n# <Set Number> - <Source Type>: <Topic>\n\n**Source:** /<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/source/<transcript_filename>\n**Source Date:** <date> (<context>)\n**Document Set:** <set number> (<description>)\n**Pass:** Focused deep dive on <topic>\n\n---\n```\n\n#### Call Preps\n\nSingle or few passes. Propose file breakdown (situational context, discovery strategy, technical reference, people). Ask user. Write files.\n\n#### Internal Debriefs\n\nSupplementary material using letter suffix (02a, 02b). Capture candid assessments honestly. Flag anything that must never appear in client-facing materials. Update org chart.\n\n#### Emails and Written Communications\n\nDetermine if new event or supplementary. Extract decisions, action items, new information. Usually fewer files than transcript sets.\n\n#### Working Discussions (User + Claude)\n\nConversation becomes a document set. Read prior context, propose discussion topics (max 5), user responds, write immediately after each exchange. Use \"continued\" files if discussion extends. Capture reasoning, not just conclusions. Distinguish hyperbole from commitments.\n\n#### Web Research During Processing\n\nWhen processing any source material, watch for unfamiliar technologies, companies, domain-specific terms, or claims needing verification:\n\n1. **Flag to the user.** \"I noticed references to Microsoft Purview, Azure AI Foundry, and SpaCy NER. Want me to spawn research agents to investigate these in parallel?\"\n2. **Spawn parallel research agents** if approved. Each agent investigates one topic and writes to `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_research_<topic>_<date>.md`.\n3. **Update glossary** if one exists at `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/glossary_<date>.md`.\n\n#### Glossary (Optional)\n\nLocated at `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/glossary_<date>.md`. Living document (editable, not append-only).\n\n**When to create:**\n- The engagement involves 3+ unfamiliar terms\n- The user asks for one\n- The engagement spans a domain BayOne has not worked in before\n\nResearch agents populate it during source processing. New terms added as discovered in subsequent sets.\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 4: CREATE DELIVERABLE\n\n**Questions to ask:**\n1. Which engagement? (if not already established)\n2. What type of deliverable?\n   - Problem restatement (demonstrates understanding, no solutions)\n   - Information request (prioritized asks, who can answer each)\n   - Preliminary approach (initial direction, explicitly preliminary)\n   - Formal proposal concise (scoped engagement with phases and pricing)\n   - Formal proposal detailed (extended version with deeper technical sections)\n   - Resource plan (staffing and team structure)\n   - Follow-up email (markdown only, maintain momentum)\n3. What is the title and cover label? (Cover labels are thematic, NOT literal. \"SECURE KNOWLEDGE ENABLEMENT\" not \"PROBLEM RESTATEMENT\".)\n4. Who is the audience? (VP level? Technical? Executive?)\n\n**Process:**\n1. Draft in markdown first in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/deliverables/<name>_<date>.md`. User reviews substance before formatting.\n2. Convert to HTML using BayOne design system. Read `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/bayone_design_spec.md` for the complete design spec. Read the appropriate gold standard for structural reference (not fill-in-the-blanks).\n3. Read `.claude/context/bayone_team.md` for cover pages and \"Why BayOne\" sections.\n4. Offer to run `/big4` for quality review before finalizing.\n5. Iterate with user until approved.\n6. Output HTML to `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/deliverables/<name>_<date>.html`.\n\n**Load references:**\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/deliverables_pipeline.md`\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/anti_patterns.md`\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/professional_standards.md`\n\n**Gold standards** (read for style and structure):\n\n| Type | Gold Standard |\n|------|--------------|\n| Problem restatement | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/problem_restatement.html` |\n| Information request | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/information_request.html` |\n| Preliminary approach | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/preliminary_approach.html` |\n| Proposal (concise) | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/poc_proposal_v5.html` |\n| Proposal (detailed) | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/poc_proposal_v5_detailed.html` |\n| Bridge document | `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/gold_standards/bridge_document_example.md` |\n\n#### Deliverable Types\n\n**Problem Restatement**\n- Purpose: Demonstrate understanding. Satisfy the \"repeat back\" requirement.\n- Contains: The problem in client's own framing, business context, specific details proving deep listening, no solutions/tech names/proposals\n- Cover label example: \"SECURE KNOWLEDGE ENABLEMENT\" (not \"PROBLEM RESTATEMENT\")\n\n**Information Request**\n- Purpose: Formally ask for what is needed to proceed\n- Contains: Prioritized list of information needs, who can answer each, specific actionable asks, next steps section\n- Cover label example: \"DISCOVERY\" (not \"INFORMATION REQUEST\")\n\n**Preliminary Approach**\n- Purpose: Present initial thinking on solution direction\n- Contains: Brief problem summary (reference, not repeat), observations on prior approaches (diplomatic), proposed architecture/methodology, enterprise tools strategy, what is needed to refine into full proposal\n- Framing: Explicitly preliminary. \"These ideas require refinement through deeper discovery.\"\n\n**Formal Proposal (Concise)**\n- Purpose: Scoped engagement with pricing, timeline, and deliverables\n- Standard structure: Cover → Challenge → Approach → Capabilities → Engagement → Why BayOne → Next Steps\n\n**Formal Proposal (Detailed)**\n- Same structure as concise but with expanded technical sections\n- Used when audience is technical\n\n**Follow-Up Emails**\n- Purpose: Keep momentum, request specific actions\n- Contains: Thanks for meeting, reference to attached documents, most important ask (prioritized), next step, flexibility on timing\n- Tone: Professional but warm, not salesy\n- Format: Markdown only, no HTML\n\n#### Client-Facing Content Rules (ABSOLUTE)\n\nThese rules apply to ALL client-facing deliverables:\n\n1. **No em dashes.** Use commas, periods, \"and,\" or parentheses instead.\n2. **No direct quotes.** Never quotation marks around something someone said. Paraphrase as organizational knowledge.\n3. **No individual names.** Frame as \"the team,\" \"the organization,\" \"Lam Research,\" or \"BayOne.\" Never \"Brad said\" or \"Mikhail mentioned.\"\n4. **No contrastive framing.** Never \"It's not just X, it's Y\" or \"This isn't about X, it's about Y.\" State what something IS directly.\n5. **No emojis.** Zero, ever.\n6. **No contractions.** \"Does not\" instead of \"doesn't,\" \"we are\" instead of \"we're.\"\n7. **Never suggest the client is incompetent.** Even if internal assessment is harsh. Frame gaps as opportunities: \"prior approaches followed a common pattern that tends to be brittle in this type of environment.\"\n8. **Do not question organizational authority in writing.** If Brad says he owns everything, the document accepts that.\n9. **Come from a position of authority without arrogance.** \"Based on BayOne's experience building similar solutions\" is good. \"Our unique and revolutionary approach\" is bad.\n10. **Call the solution a \"solution,\" never a \"product.\"** It is a custom, tailor-made engagement, not an off-the-shelf product.\n11. **Stay within the stated problem.** Do not pitch future growth or expansion beyond a brief mention in passing.\n12. **Preliminary work should be framed as preliminary.** \"These are initial ideas informed by our experience. They require refinement through deeper discovery.\"\n13. **Acknowledge what you do not know.** If specific information is needed before a recommendation can be finalized, say so explicitly.\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 5: PRICING\n\n**When to offer:** After research is complete and scope is understood. Not before.\n\n**Process:**\n\n1. Run structured questionnaire in batches:\n   - **Batch 1: Scope and Revenue** - Deliverable, unit of measurement, quoted price/expected revenue, hours per unit, POC absorption\n   - **Batch 2: Team and Costs** - Team members (names, roles, salary/rate), load factor (default 20%), user's allocation percentage, shared resources\n   - **Batch 3: Timeline and Scenarios** - Delivery date options, development/stabilization months per scenario, team size changes, allocation changes\n   - **Batch 4: Margin and Risk** - Target margin (e.g., 40% ideal, 30% floor), risk reserve percentage, travel costs, hardware/consumables\n   - **Batch 5: Discount Strategy** - Likelihood of discount request, absolute floor margin, discount application rules\n\n2. Prototype numbers in the conversation first.\n\n3. Build pricing spec markdown in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/pricing/pricing_spec_<date>.md`. This file doubles as a prompt for Claude in Excel.\n\n4. **Capture the pricing discussion as a research set.** The reasoning behind pricing decisions (margin targets, team composition, discount strategy) goes in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_discussion_pricing_strategy_<date>.md`. The pricing spec is the actionable output. The research doc is the record of how and why.\n\n5. Reference the template at `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/templates/ProjectCostingTemplate.xlsx` and the creation prompt at `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/prompts/excel_template_prompt.md`.\n\n6. Write correction prompts as separate dated files in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/pricing/` if iteration is needed.\n\n**Load references:**\n- `.claude/skills/singularity/references/pricing_workflow.md`\n\n**Pricing Spec Output:**\nThe pricing spec contains:\n- All input parameters (rates, allocations, load factors, hours per unit)\n- Month-by-month cost breakdowns for each scenario\n- Risk reserve calculations\n- Margin analysis at base cost and loaded cost\n- Discount sensitivity table (if applicable)\n- Per-unit economics (cost per screen, margin per screen, etc.)\n- Throughput validation (can the team actually deliver the scope in the timeline?)\n\n**Cross-Session Pattern:**\nPricing specs become prompts for Claude in Excel sessions. If iteration is needed, write correction prompts as separate markdown files:\n\n```\n/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/pricing/\n├── pricing_spec_2026-03-26.md                  (main spec)\n├── pricing_corrections_v1_2026-03-26.md        (first round feedback)\n├── pricing_corrections_v2_2026-03-27.md        (second round)\n└── ...\n```\n\n---\n\n### FLOW 6: DISCUSSION\n\n**When to proactively offer:** After 2+ document sets exist. Say: \"We have enough context to discuss BayOne's positioning for this engagement. Want to work through strategy before we draft deliverables?\"\n\n**Standard strategy questions (present as first batch):**\n1. What does BayOne want from this engagement? (Staffing? Solutions? Advisory? Long-term relationship?)\n2. What is BayOne's unique angle or credibility for this specific client?\n3. Who has decision-making authority on the client side? Who influences but does not decide?\n4. What are the explicit asks vs. implicit needs?\n5. What is the competitive landscape? Who else is the client talking to?\n\n**Process:**\n1. Read prior context (latest summaries + org chart)\n2. Propose discussion topics based on open threads from research\n3. User responds\n4. Write immediately after each exchange to `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_discussion_<topic>_<date>.md`. Do not accumulate.\n5. Continue in batches of max 5 questions\n6. Write summary when user indicates discussion is complete\n\n**Special rules for discussions:**\n- Capture the user's reasoning, not just conclusions. The \"why\" is as valuable as the \"what.\"\n- Flag open items explicitly. If the user says \"this requires discovery\" or \"we need info from the sales team,\" that is an open item.\n- Do not sanitize. Capture blunt assessments, strong opinions, and confidence claims honestly.\n- Distinguish hyperbole from commitments. \"I could do this in a day\" is confidence in feasibility, not a timeline commitment. Do not repeat it as a planning estimate.\n\n---\n\n## AGENT ARCHITECTURE\n\n### When to Use Agents\n\n**Use Agents For:**\n- Per-topic deep dives (after topic map is created and approved)\n- Research lookups (external information needed in context of problem)\n- Re-exploration of existing docs (find specific details across multiple files)\n\n**Do Not Use Agents For:**\n- The people file (requires reading org chart first, updating after)\n- The topic map (produces list for agents)\n- The summary document (synthesizes across all files)\n- The bridge document (requires reading across two sets)\n- The org chart update (requires reading current org chart + all per-set people info)\n- Deliverables (require synthesis and user interaction)\n\n### Permission Setup (CRITICAL)\n\nThe following MUST be in `.claude/settings.local.json`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"permissions\": {\n    \"allow\": [\n      \"Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/**)\"\n    ]\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThis grants agents write access to the entire project directory. With this permission and `mode: \"bypassPermissions\"` on agent spawns, agents write files fully autonomously with zero user approval prompts.\n\n**Mandatory Startup Check:** Every time singularity is invoked, before doing ANY work, verify:\n1. Read `.claude/settings.local.json`\n2. Verify that `permissions.allow` contains a Write permission that covers the project root. Acceptable patterns:\n   - `Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/**)` (preferred)\n   - `Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/<specific_client>/**)` (acceptable if client folder known)\n3. If missing or too narrow:\n   - Tell user: \"Singularity requires agents to write files autonomously. The Write permission in `.claude/settings.local.json` needs to cover the engagement folder. I need to add `Write($CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/**)` to the permissions. May I do that?\"\n   - If approved, add it; if declined, explain skill cannot function and stop\n\n### Parallel Execution\n\nAll per-topic agents should be spawned in a single message with multiple tool calls. This maximizes parallelism. The agents run concurrently and return results as they complete.\n\n### Agent Names\n\nGive each agent a short, descriptive name in the `description` field:\n- \"Deep dive: technical use cases\"\n- \"Deep dive: what was tried\"\n- \"Deep dive: infrastructure and access\"\n\n### Post-Agent Workflow\n\nAfter all agents complete:\n1. Read all agent output files\n2. Write the synthesis files (summary, bridge document, org chart update)\n3. Inform the user of what was created and current state\n\n### Error Handling\n\nIf an agent fails to write a file:\n- Do NOT fall back to writing the file in the main session\n- Do NOT attempt to extract agent output from temp files\n- Report the failure and ask the user how to proceed\n\n### Research Agents (Distinct from Deep-Dive Agents)\n\nResearch agents perform external research using WebSearch and WebFetch. They write findings directly to the engagement folder.\n\n**What Research Agents Do:**\n- Technology research (unfamiliar technologies, frameworks, platforms, services)\n- Company research (client companies, competitors, market position, news)\n- Benchmark research (industry benchmarks, market rates, competitive pricing, best practices)\n- Validation (verify technical claims against current documentation)\n- Domain exploration (understand unfamiliar domains the client operates in)\n\n**Where Research Agents Write:**\n- Technology and domain research → `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/<set>_research_<topic>_<date>.md`\n- Glossary updates → `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/glossary_<date>.md`\n- Benchmark data → `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/pricing/benchmarks_<topic>_<date>.md` (pricing-related)\n\n**Parallel Research:**\nMultiple research agents can run in parallel when there are several independent topics to investigate.\n\n**Research Agent Prompt Template:**\n\n```\nYou are a research agent for a consulting engagement. Your job is to deeply\ninvestigate a specific topic and write a comprehensive research document.\n\n**Your research topic:** [TOPIC]\n\n**Context from the engagement:** [BRIEF CONTEXT about why this topic matters]\n\n**Research instructions:**\n1. Use WebSearch to find current, authoritative information\n2. Focus on: capabilities, limitations, pricing (if relevant), how it relates\n   to the engagement context\n3. Distinguish between facts and marketing claims\n4. Note the date of your sources (prefer recent over old)\n\n**Write your findings to:** [exact file path]\n\nUse this header format:\n# <Set Number> - Research: <Topic>\n\n**Source:** Web research\n**Date:** <today's date>\n**Document Set:** <set number>\n**Context:** <why this research was needed>\n\n---\n\n[Your findings here]\n```\n\n**When to Launch Research Agents:**\n- During source processing (when unfamiliar terms/technologies encountered)\n- During discussion mode (when topics need external validation)\n- During pricing (when market benchmarks needed)\n- During deliverable creation (when claims need verification)\n- Proactively (when unfamiliar terms detected)\n\n**Research Output Quality:**\n- Cite sources (URLs or document names)\n- Distinguish between verified facts and claims\n- Note when information may be outdated\n- Flag conflicting information from different sources\n- Write in professional, detailed style\n\n---\n\n## PEOPLE TRACKING SYSTEM\n\n### Dual System Design\n\nPeople information is tracked in two places simultaneously. This is intentional and serves different purposes.\n\n### 1. Per-Set People Documents (Blockchain Style)\n\nLocated in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/`, these capture what was learned about people from a specific source event. They are append-only, like all research documents.\n\n**Example:** `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/research/02_meeting_people_2026-03-12.md`\n\n**What they capture:**\n- Who was present (or mentioned) in that specific event\n- Roles and titles as stated or observed\n- Sentiment and behavioral observations from that event\n- Relationship dynamics observed in that event\n- New people introduced for the first time\n- Candid assessments (for internal debriefs)\n\n**Why they exist:** They preserve the historical record of when information was learned. If you want to know \"what did we think about Brad after the first meeting vs. after the debrief,\" you read the Set 02 people doc and then the Set 02a people doc.\n\n### 2. Living Org Chart (Always Current)\n\nLocated at `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md`, this always reflects the most current and complete understanding of all people across all events.\n\n**What it contains:**\n- Every person known to the engagement\n- Their most current title, role, and relationship to the engagement\n- A \"Known Since\" field indicating which set introduced them\n- Sentiment notes that reflect the cumulative understanding\n- A relationship map section at the bottom\n\n**Why it exists:** It is the quick-reference truth. A new session reads the org chart and immediately knows who everyone is, what they do, and how they relate to each other.\n\n**This is the one exception to append-only.** The org chart IS updated over time because its purpose is to be a current-state reference, not a historical record.\n\n### Org Chart Structure\n\n```markdown\n# [Engagement Name] - Org Chart\n\n**Last Updated:** [date] (from document set [number])\n\n---\n\n## [Client Company Name]\n\n### [Person Name] (\"[Nickname]\")\n- **Title:** [Title]\n- **Role in Engagement:** [Their role]\n- **Ownership:** [What they own, if relevant]\n- **Background:** [Career background if known]\n- **Sentiment:** [Behavioral observations, communication style, trust signals]\n- **Key Quote:** [One defining quote, if available]\n- **Working Style:** [How they work, what they expect]\n- **Known Since:** Set [number] ([source description])\n\n### [Next Person]\n...\n\n---\n\n## [BayOne Solutions / Our Company]\n\n### [Person Name]\n- **Title:** [Title]\n- **Role in Engagement:** [Their role]\n- **Relevant Experience:** [If applicable]\n- **Meeting Performance:** [How they performed in the meeting, if applicable]\n- **Known Since:** Set [number]\n\n---\n\n## Relationship Map\n\n- **[Key relationship 1]**\n- **[Key relationship 2]**\n- **[Competitive landscape if known]**\n- **[Next engagement point]**\n```\n\n### Workflow: Processing People Information\n\n**Before Processing a New Set:**\n1. Read `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` to have full context on all known people\n2. Read the prior set's summary for context on where we left off\n\n**During Processing:**\n3. Create the per-set people document as the first file for transcript-based sets\n4. Capture everything specific to this event: who was there, what was observed, sentiment in this specific interaction\n\n**After All Set Files Are Written:**\n5. Update `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` with any new people, updated titles, new relationship information, or changed sentiment based on this set\n6. Update the \"Last Updated\" line in `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/org_chart.md` to reflect the current set\n\n### What to Capture About People\n\n**For Client-Side People:**\n\n| Field | What to Include |\n|-------|----------------|\n| Title | Official title as stated or discovered |\n| Role in Engagement | What they do in the context of this initiative |\n| Ownership | What they control (budget, decisions, systems, team) |\n| Background | Career progression if known |\n| Sentiment | Communication style, trust signals, red flags, behavioral patterns |\n| Key Quote | One quote that captures their personality or priorities |\n| Working Style | How they expect things to work (requirements, engagement model) |\n| Relationship to Others | Who they defer to, who defers to them, tensions, alignments |\n\n**For Internal People:**\n\n| Field | What to Include |\n|-------|----------------|\n| Title | Official title |\n| Role in Engagement | What they do in the context of this initiative |\n| Relevant Experience | Why they are on this engagement |\n| Meeting Performance | What landed well, what got redirected, strengths and weaknesses observed |\n| Relationship to Others | Internal dynamics, who drives what |\n\n**For People Mentioned but Not Met:**\n\n| Field | What to Include |\n|-------|----------------|\n| Title | If known |\n| Role | Expected role when they join |\n| Context | How they were referenced and by whom |\n| Significance | Why they matter to the engagement |\n\n### Handling Candid Assessments\n\nInternal debriefs often contain blunt, unfiltered opinions about client team members. These are valuable and should be captured honestly in the per-set people documents and reflected (more diplomatically) in the org chart.\n\n**Rules:**\n- Internal debrief people docs should preserve the tone and candor of the assessment\n- The org chart should reflect the substance of the assessment without inflammatory language\n- Flag any assessment that could be colored by ego friction or frustration\n- Never let internal candid assessments leak into client-facing deliverables\n\n### Competitors and External Parties\n\nCompetitors, prior partners, and other external parties mentioned in meetings should be tracked in the org chart's relationship map section, not as individual entries. They are contextual, not stakeholders.\n\n---\n\n## SESSION CONTINUITY\n\n### The Problem\n\nClaude Code sessions have finite context. Engagements may span weeks or months. Multiple sessions will work on the same engagement. Each new session starts with zero context about the engagement.\n\nThe blockchain methodology solves this by making the document system self-describing. But the skill also needs operational continuity: how does a new session know where the previous one left off, what was completed, and what still needs to be done?\n\n### Session Handoff Documents\n\nWhen a session ends with incomplete work, create a handoff document:\n\n```\n/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/session_handoff_<date>.md\n```\n\n**What a Handoff Document Contains:**\n\n1. **What was completed this session.** Which document sets were fully processed, which files were written.\n\n2. **What is partially completed.** Which files exist, which are still needed. Include exact file names that were planned but not written.\n\n3. **What needs to happen next.** Specific, actionable next steps with enough context that a new session can pick up without re-reading everything.\n\n4. **Known issues or blockers.** Anything that went wrong, workarounds that were applied, things to avoid.\n\n5. **User preferences learned.** Any guidance the user gave that is not captured in the methodology or skill notes.\n\n6. **File inventory.** A tree listing of all files in the engagement folder with their status (DONE, IN PROGRESS, NOT STARTED, DELETE).\n\n### Reading Order for a New Session\n\nWhen a new session begins on an existing engagement:\n\n1. **Read the handoff document** (if one exists) to understand where things left off.\n2. **Read the methodology document** to understand the system.\n3. **Read the skill notes** (if they exist) to understand accumulated do's and don'ts.\n4. **Read the summary documents** in order to reconstruct the engagement state.\n5. **Read the org chart** for people context.\n6. **Read any incomplete work** that needs to be resumed.\n\n### Skill Notes as Accumulated Wisdom\n\nThe `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/planning/skill_notes.md` file accumulates do's, don'ts, and wisdom across sessions. It is organized by topic and is NOT append-only—it can be updated as guidance evolves.\n\nWhen the user gives guidance during a session (\"don't fixate on timeline claims,\" \"max 5 questions per batch\"), it should be captured in skill notes immediately, not at the end of the session.\n\n---\n\n## QUALITY STANDARDS AND RULES\n\n### Professional Standards (Big Four Consulting Quality)\n\n**Document Structure:**\n- Executive Summary (2-3 paragraphs max)\n- Problem Statement\n- Proposed Approach\n- Scope and Timeline\n- Investment/Pricing\n- Assumptions and Dependencies\n- Risk Factors\n- Next Steps\n\n**Voice and Tone:**\n- Organizational voice throughout (not first-person)\n- Confident without arrogant\n- Direct statements (no \"it could be argued that...\")\n- Demonstrate understanding with specific details\n- Concrete over abstract\n\n**Headers and Sections:**\n- Noun phrases preferred\n- No punctuation in headers\n- Parallel structure across headers at the same level\n\n**Technical Content:**\n- Show understanding of specific technology\n- Concrete over abstract examples\n- Qualify appropriately when uncertain\n- Include explicit success criteria\n- Clear scope boundaries (included AND excluded)\n\n**Tables and Lists:**\n- Use tables for comparisons, mappings, phase breakdowns\n- Numbered lists for sequential steps\n- Bulleted lists for non-sequential items\n- Parallel structure across items\n\n### Anti-Patterns to Detect and Fix\n\n**1. Contrastive Rhetorical Framing** (Most Common AI Tell)\n- \"This isn't X, it's Y\" → State what something IS directly\n- \"It's not just X, it's Y\" → Remove negative framing entirely\n- \"The challenge isn't X. The challenge is Y.\" → Reverse to positive statement\n\n**2. Em-Dash Overuse**\n- Replace 90% of em-dashes with commas, parentheses, or periods\n- Reserve dashes for true interruptions\n- Max 5 em-dashes in entire document\n\n**3. Rhetorical Questions with Fragment Answers**\n- \"The result? [Fragment].\" → \"The result was [complete statement].\"\n- Make statements instead of interviewing yourself\n\n**4. Triple Patterns**\n- \"Fast. Reliable. Secure.\" → Use pairs, singles, or longer lists\n- Avoid AI's obsession with grouping things in threes\n\n**5. First Person in Business Documents**\n- \"I will...\" → \"BayOne will...\"\n- Use organizational voice, not personal pronouns\n\n**6. Blog-Style Headers**\n- \"Why This Matters\" → \"Business Impact\"\n- \"The Flywheel Effect\" → \"Acceleration Mechanism\"\n- Use formal, descriptive headers\n\n**7. Colloquial Language**\n- \"skinning exercise\" → \"surface-level UI modification\"\n- \"baked into\" → \"embedded within\"\n- \"heavy lifting\" → \"foundational work\"\n- \"Happy to discuss\" → \"BayOne welcomes the opportunity to discuss\"\n\n**8. Inspirational Pivots**\n- Shift from specific to abstract to create fake profundity\n- Stay concrete, avoid grand abstractions\n\n**9. Universal Claims Without Evidence**\n- \"Studies show that...\" → \"In our 2024 client survey...\"\n- Cite real sources or avoid universal claims\n\n**10. Filler Words**\n- \"just\", \"actually\", \"really\", \"very\", \"truly\" → Delete these\n- They add nothing\n\n**11. Emoji Usage**\n- Zero emojis in formal documents\n- Maximum one in casual contexts (rare)\n\n**12. Contractions in Formal Documents**\n- \"isn't\" → \"is not\"\n- \"we're\" → \"we are\"\n- \"doesn't\" → \"does not\"\n\n**The Ultimate Test:**\n1. Would this sound natural if read aloud to the client?\n2. Are there specific details demonstrating real understanding?\n3. Does it sound like a professional on Tuesday, not someone giving a TED talk?\n4. Have all em-dashes, rhetorical questions, and \"it's not just\" phrases been removed?\n5. Is every claim supported by specific evidence?\n6. Would a Big Four consultant be embarrassed to send this?\n\n---\n\n## SCRIPTS AND UTILITIES\n\n### singularity_stop.py\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/scripts/singularity_stop.py`\n\n**Purpose:** A Stop hook (skill-scoped) that verifies engagement artifacts exist using the Proof via Artifact pattern.\n\n**What it does:**\n1. Finds engagement folders by looking for `research/00_methodology_*.md`\n2. Checks that org_chart.md exists (if research has progressed beyond methodology)\n3. Checks that summary documents exist (if detail files exist)\n4. Reports issues to stderr and exits with status codes\n\n**When it fires:** Only when singularity is active (skill-scoped, not global)\n\n### html_to_pdf.py\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/scripts/html_to_pdf.py`\n\n**Purpose:** Converts HTML documents (proposals and slides) to PDF format.\n\n**Features:**\n- Multiple rendering engines (Chrome headless or WeasyPrint)\n- Single file or directory processing\n- PDF merging for multi-page documents\n- Landscape orientation support\n- Cross-platform (Linux, macOS, Windows via WSL)\n\n**Usage:**\n```bash\npython html_to_pdf.py proposal.html\npython html_to_pdf.py slides/ deck.pdf --merge --landscape\npython html_to_pdf.py input.html output.pdf --engine weasyprint\n```\n\n---\n\n## ASSETS AND TEMPLATES\n\n### Templates\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/templates/`\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `methodology_template.md` | Template for the 00_methodology document. Fill in client name and opportunity description. |\n| `proposal_template.html` | Structural reference showing standard section order and CSS patterns. NOT a fill-in-the-blanks template. |\n| `ProjectCostingTemplate.xlsx` | Ready-to-use Excel costing workbook with formulas, validation, and dummy data. |\n\n### Prompts\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/prompts/`\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `excel_template_prompt.md` | Complete specification for Claude in Excel to recreate the .xlsx from scratch. Includes dummy data, formulas, formatting, and validation rules. |\n\n### Design System\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/assets/design/`\n\n| File | Purpose |\n|------|---------|\n| `bayone_design_spec.md` | Complete BayOne design system specification including color palette, typography, components, and patterns. |\n| `gold_standards/problem_restatement.html` | Example from Lam Research showing full design system implementation |\n| `gold_standards/information_request.html` | Example with priority tiers, ask cards, CTA boxes |\n| `gold_standards/preliminary_approach.html` | Example with technical content presentation |\n| `gold_standards/poc_proposal_v5.html` | Standard proposal structure |\n| `gold_standards/poc_proposal_v5_detailed.html` | Extended proposal with deeper technical sections |\n| `gold_standards/bridge_document_example.md` | Gold standard format for bridge documents |\n\n---\n\n## REFERENCES DIRECTORY: COMPLETE LIST\n\n**Location:** `.claude/skills/singularity/references/`\n\n1. **blockchain_methodology.md** - Core append-only methodology, numbering conventions, standard files per set, reading order for new sessions\n\n2. **document_processing.md** - How to process each source type (transcripts, call preps, debriefs, working discussions, emails), multi-pass reading, web research during processing, glossary creation\n\n3. **folder_structure.md** - Engagement folder specification, file naming conventions, bridge documents, deliverable files, document header format\n\n4. **people_tracking.md** - Dual system (per-set docs + org chart), org chart structure, workflow for processing people info, what to capture, handling candid assessments\n\n5. **agent_architecture.md** - Pricing questionnaire structure (5 batches), building pricing spec, handoff to Excel, iteration workflow, cross-session artifact pattern\n\n8. **session_continuity.md** - Session handoff documents, reading order for new sessions, methodology document as anchor, skill notes as accumulated wisdom\n\n9. **anti_patterns.md** - 12 AI writing anti-patterns to detect and fix with examples and professional alternatives\n\n10. **professional_standards.md** - Big Four consulting quality standards (structure, voice, tone, headers, technical content, tables, lists, closings)\n\n11. **complete_structure.md** - The system is designed as a flat structure where:\n\n- Each engagement has one root folder `/<client_name>/<opportunity_name>/`\n- All document sets within that engagement are numbered sequentially (01, 02, 03...)\n- Supplementary materials use letter suffixes (02a, 02b, 02c) within the same event\n\nIf nested singularities were needed (e.g., a team sub-engagement inside a main engagement), they would likely follow the same blockchain methodology but use a nested folder structure:\n\n```\n/<client_name>/<main_opportunity>/\n├── team_a_sub_engagement/\n│   ├── research/\n│   ├── source/\n│   └── ...\n└── team_b_sub_engagement/\n    ├── research/\n    ├── source/\n    └── ...\n```\n\nHowever, this is NOT explicitly documented in the current skill, so confirmation from the skill owner would be needed.\n\n---\n\n## INVOCATION WORKFLOW\n\nWhen the user invokes `/singularity`, ask:\n\n> **What are we doing today?**\n> 1. **New engagement** - organize a new client opportunity from scratch\n> 2. **Continue** - pick up where we left off on an existing engagement\n> 3. **Process source** - add a new transcript, email, or document to an existing engagement\n> 4. **Deliver** - draft a client-facing document\n> 5. **Price** - build or refine a pricing model\n> 6. **Discuss** - think through strategy or technical approach\n\nIf the user provides context that makes the intent obvious, skip this question and go directly to the right flow.\n\n---\n\nThis comprehensive specification provides the complete foundation for understanding and auditing whether an existing client folder conforms to Singularity structure and practices."

# SINGULARITY SKILL + CISCO/CICD FOLDER AUDIT

## PART 1: WHAT SINGULARITY IS AND HOW IT WORKS

**Core Concept**: Singularity is a consulting engagement orchestrator that transforms raw transcripts, emails, and call preps into a structured research library, client deliverables, and pricing models. It replaces the older sales-forge skill.

**Key Architectural Principles:**
1. **Blockchain methodology** — Research docs are numbered chronologically (`01_...`, `02_...`, `02a_...`) and are **append-only/immutable**. Only `org_chart.md` and `00_methodology_*.md` may be updated in place.
2. **Multi-pass processing** — Transcripts get read repeatedly, each pass focused on one topic. Parallel agents spawn for topical deep dives.
3. **Everything is dated** (YYYY-MM-DD) using the **source material date**, not analysis date.
4. **Propose before writing** — Always propose file lists to the user before spawning agents or writing files.

**Canonical Folder Structure (per engagement at `/<client>/<opportunity>/`):**
```
org_chart.md              ← living, always-current people/sentiment map
source/                   ← raw inputs, NEVER modified
research/                 ← blockchain decomposition (append-only)
  00_methodology_*.md     ← the one research doc that IS editable
  01_*, 02_*, 02a_*, ...  ← chronological sets + letter-suffix supplements
  01-02_changes_*.md      ← bridge documents between sets
planning/                 ← handoffs, skill_notes, glossary (editable)
pricing/                  ← pricing specs + correction prompts (versioned)
deliverables/             ← client-facing MD + HTML (flat, dated)
presentations/            ← slide decks (dated HTML)
decisions/                ← open questions + agreed decisions
progress/                 ← running status
```

**Standard files per set:** people, topic map, per-topic deep dives, **summary (always last)**. Bridge documents get written retrospectively after the next set exists.

**Content rules for client-facing deliverables**: no em dashes, no contractions, no direct quotes, no individual names, no contrastive framing ("not X, but Y"), no emojis, "solution never product."

**Sub-Singularity Concept**: Notably, the skill docs do **NOT explicitly define** sub-singularities. The Explorer agent flagged this: nested singularities aren't a first-class feature of the skill. They're a convention being used on the ground (same blockchain/folder pattern, nested).

**Sibling skills**: big4 (quality review), pdf-extractor, pptx-extractor.

---

## PART 2: CURRENT STATE OF `cisco/cicd/`

**Scale**: 526 files across 55 directories. Engagement runs Nov 2025 → present, contract renewal Apr 30.

### Engagement Narrative

- **Client**: Cisco Cloud Networking Group. Build an AI-driven CI/CD assistant on top of Cisco's **DeepSight Atlas** platform.
- **Problem**: The NxOS CI/CD workflow WebEx space gets 4,200+ actionable messages/quarter across 25 issue categories. Only 34% of questions get responses; test failure avg response time is 5.4 hours.
- **Solution tracks**: (1) WebEx scraper for user pain point analysis (Srikar lead), (2) Build log understanding (Namita lead, now suspended).
- **Team**: Colin (lead) + Srikar (on-site) + Namita (on-site, suspended) + Saurav (offshore, built Wall-E bot) + Askari (near-silent) + Vaishali & Tanuja (new, onboarding Apr 17).
- **Cisco stakeholders**: Anand (exec sponsor), Srinivas (technical visionary), Divakar (access), Justin (build infra), Naga (Pulse/Scribble), Rui (Nexus T — scope conflict), Anupma (CAT DB), Shih-Ta (onboarding), Matt Healy (CSIRT).

### Current Phase: **Active Crisis Management**

**Apr 20 incident**: Namita committed 4 data-handling violations (shared source code with Vaishali on Teams, blocked zip transfer attempt, AirDropped presentation from Cisco laptop to BayOne laptop, uploaded `Log_type_mapping.pdf` to BayOne GitHub). Cisco CSIRT (Matt Healy) opened formal investigation. Namita's Cisco access suspended. Contract renewal (Apr 30) contingent on GPS findings.

**Incident Set 06–06g** is the most recent documentation: 8 chronological sub-files covering disclosure facts, Colin's response to Namita, CSIRT exchange, internal prep call, Anand briefing, Yogesh CEO call, Neha/Zahra commercial layer, and Namita follow-up (Apr 21, most recent).

### Folder Layout — Conformance to Singularity

**Conforming:**
- `research/` — 47 markdown files, Sets 01–10 with proper blockchain numbering + change bridges. Through Apr 6.
- `source/` — 16 raw inputs preserved, never modified.
- `planning/` — Session handoffs and briefing outlines dated correctly.
- `deliverables/` — Srinivas primer + team briefing HTML, flat and dated.
- `presentations/` — Two dated status decks (Apr 10, Apr 17), 8 slides each.
- `documents/` — PDF extractions (Cisco EPNM guide extracted via pdf-extractor pattern).
- `org_chart.md` — Comprehensive, 25+ stakeholders, updated through Apr 21 with incident impact on sentiment.
- `archive/` — Pre-restructure historical content cleanly segregated (SOW work, communications sub-engagement, meeting breakdowns, resource planning, JDs).

**Deviations / Anomalies:**
- **Empty placeholders at root**: `decisions/`, `pricing/`, `progress/` exist but are unused. Pricing never activated.
- **`inventory/` folder** (5 self-generated files: folder_descriptions, master_map, markdown_inventory, non_markdown_inventory, sub_team) — this is NOT standard Singularity; it's a custom audit/navigation aid.
- **No root-level decisions.md or progress tracking**; these live only in the team sub-singularity.

### The Team Sub-Singularity (`cisco/cicd/team/`)

This is a **nested Singularity** tracking the active BayOne team's daily ops. It follows Singularity structure faithfully but adds operational tracking not standard in the parent skill:

```
team/
├── research/        ← 25 MD files: Sets 01, 02, 02a-02e, 06-06g (incident)
├── source/          ← dated weekly/daily/person structure
│   ├── week_2026-04-14/day_2026-04-16/{namita,saurav,srikar,vaishali}/
│   ├── week_2026-04-14/day_2026-04-17/srikar/
│   └── week_2026-04-20/day_{2026-04-20,2026-04-21}/
├── tracking/        ← action_items.md (38), blockers.md (20), decisions.md
├── documents/       ← empty
├── planning/        ← empty
└── cross_reference.md
```

**Notable patterns:**
- **Source structure is unusual**: `week_/day_/person_/` nesting is a custom adaptation not in the base Singularity pattern. It's working well for tracking per-person contributions.
- **`tracking/` folder is novel** — action_items/blockers/decisions as live operational docs, not a Singularity default.
- **Incident Sets 06–06g** — 8 related files for one event cascade (disclosure + 6 call follow-ups + 1 post-meeting exchange). This is an extension of the letter-suffix convention (normally 02a/02b), now used at scale.
- **Empty `vaishali/` source folder** — she was added Apr 17 but no source captured yet.

### Flags and Risks

- **Askari disengagement** — 1 message in 16 days, 2.6% of team chat activity. Role fit unclear.
- **DeepSight access blocked 4 weeks** — core platform access still pending; Colin escalating.
- **Scope conflicts unresolved** — Rui's Nexus T (failure analysis agent) and Naga's Pulse/Scribble both overlap with BayOne's work territory.
- **Single points of failure** — Divakar (access provisioning), Srinivas (technical vision), Anand (budget).
- **Renewal readiness uncertain** — Apr 30 deadline. No code in production yet. Incident outcome will be deciding factor.

### Last Activity

**Most recent file**: `team/research/06g_incident_namita_post_meeting_exchange_2026-04-21.md`. 3 days old.

## SUMMARY

The Cisco CICD folder is a **mature, well-organized Singularity engagement** with a fully functional nested team sub-singularity. Structure is clean, blockchain discipline is held through Set 10 (main) and Sets 01-06g (team). The custom `inventory/`, `tracking/`, and `source/week/day/person/` patterns are adaptations on top of Singularity — not defects. Current state: **active incident response**, awaiting Cisco GPS findings, contract renewal pending Apr 30.

Standing by for the other skill you want to use.

# FIDO — Fetch It, Deliver Output

Retrieve Microsoft Teams meeting transcripts via Graph API. Standalone — no Django, no database.

## Hard Rules

1. **Check dependencies first.** Run `python3 {baseDir}/scripts/check_deps.py` before any operation. If it fails, tell user to run `pip install -r {baseDir}/scripts/requirements.txt`.
2. **Check auth before any API call.** Run `python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py check-auth --env-file <path>`. If it fails, guide user through auth setup.
3. **Never store secrets in skill files.** API keys, tokens, and credentials live ONLY in the user's .env file and token_cache.json. No exceptions.
4. **Ask, don't churn.** If a person's email is unknown, ASK the user. If a meeting can't be found, REPORT what was searched and ask for clarification. Never guess.
5. **Smart timing for missing transcripts.** When no transcript is found, check how long ago the meeting ended:
   - < 30 min ago: "Transcript may still be processing. Try again in 15-20 minutes."
   - 30-90 min ago: "Not yet available. If transcription was enabled, try again shortly."
   - > 90 min ago: "Transcription was likely not enabled for this meeting."
6. **Scripts run with python3, never poetry.** All invocations use `python3`, not `poetry run`.
7. **Save contacts after resolution.** When a new person/email is resolved, save to contacts.json immediately.
8. **Number same-day meetings.** Multiple meetings on the same day with the same person get `_01`, `_02`, etc. ordered chronologically.
9. **Env file location must be provided by user.** Always ask the user where their .env file is. Never assume a location.
10. **Token cache and contacts must be gitignored.** Warn user if these files could be tracked by git.
11. **Never make changes without user approval.** Do not decide on a "simpler path" and act on it. When there are options (auth flows, config changes, troubleshooting approaches), present all options to the user and let them choose. You do not get to make these decisions.
12. **Troubleshoot auth failures using the reference doc.** When auth fails, read `{baseDir}/references/auth_troubleshooting.md` and walk the user through their options. Do not guess at fixes.
13. **Deduplication.** Already-downloaded transcripts are skipped automatically. Do not re-fetch unless user explicitly asks.

## Prerequisites

### Step 1: Check Dependencies

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/check_deps.py
```

If missing packages, tell the user:
```bash
pip install -r {baseDir}/scripts/requirements.txt
```

### Step 2: Environment File

Ask the user for the path to their `.env` file. Required variables:

| Variable | Description | Required |
|----------|-------------|----------|
| `MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID` | Azure app registration client ID | Yes |
| `MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET` (or `MICROSOFT_SECRET`) | Azure app registration secret | Yes |
| `AZURE_TENANT_ID` | Azure AD tenant ID | Yes |
| `GRAPH_API_SCOPES` | Comma-separated scopes | No (defaults provided) |
| `MICROSOFT_USER_ID` | Azure AD user object ID (client credentials only) | Only for client creds |
| `DEFAULT_SEARCH_DAYS` | How far back to search | No (default: 30) |

Show them the template at `{baseDir}/.env.example` if they need to create one.

### Step 3: Authentication

One-time setup — device code flow (recommended):

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/auth_bootstrap.py --env-file /path/to/.env
```

Prints a URL and code. User opens URL in their Windows browser and enters the code.
Token cached to `claude/meeting_transcripts/token_cache.json`. Valid for ~90 days.

Requires "Allow public client flows" = Yes in Azure Portal > App Registration > Authentication.

For client credentials flow (fully unattended, requires admin setup):
```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/auth_bootstrap.py --env-file /path/to/.env --client-credentials
```

## Operations

### Fetch Transcripts with a Specific Person

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py fetch \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --person "email@example.com" \
  --start-date 2026-04-01 \
  --end-date 2026-04-16
```

Fetch the most recent meeting only:
```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py fetch \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --person "email@example.com" \
  --last
```

**Exclusive mode** — only 1:1 meetings (no group meetings):
```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py fetch \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --person "Shalini" \
  --exclusive \
  --start-date 2025-12-01 --end-date 2026-04-16
```

`--only` is shorthand for `--exclusive`. `--inclusive` is the default (any meeting where the person is an attendee, even group meetings).

The `--person` flag accepts an email address OR a name from the contacts glossary.

### Fetch ALL Transcripts in a Date Range

No `--person` flag = fetch all meetings with transcripts:

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py fetch \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --start-date 2026-04-10 \
  --end-date 2026-04-16
```

### Search Meetings by Name

Find meetings by subject, then optionally fetch a transcript:

```bash
# Search only (shows results, no fetch)
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py search \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --query "Cisco Team Sync" \
  --start-date 2026-04-16 --end-date 2026-04-16

# Search and fetch transcript
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py search \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --query "Sprint Review" \
  --start-date 2026-04-01 --end-date 2026-04-16 \
  --fetch
```

When `--fetch` is used: if one match, auto-selects it. If multiple, prompts user to choose.
Search results show subject, date/time, duration, attendees, and online status.
**Present results to the user first** — do not auto-fetch without `--fetch`.

### List Meetings

Returns a formatted table of meetings — subjects, attendees, times, durations:

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py list \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --start-date 2026-04-10 \
  --end-date 2026-04-16
```

**Export to file:**
```bash
# Save as CSV
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py list \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --start-date 2026-04-10 --end-date 2026-04-16 \
  --save --format csv

# Save as markdown
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py list \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --start-date 2026-04-10 --end-date 2026-04-16 \
  --save --format md
```

Files saved to `claude/meeting_transcripts/exports/`.

### Meeting Statistics

Summary stats for a date range — meeting count, hours, top attendees, day-of-week breakdown:

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py stats \
  --env-file /path/to/.env \
  --start-date 2026-03-17 \
  --end-date 2026-04-16
```

Default range is last 30 days if no dates specified.

### Upcoming Meetings (Calendar Overview)

"What's on my calendar?" — grouped by day with attendees and online status:

```bash
# Next 7 days (default)
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py upcoming --env-file /path/to/.env

# Next 3 days
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py upcoming --env-file /path/to/.env --days 3
```

### Contact Management

```bash
# Look up a person by name
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py contacts --lookup "Ambar"

# Add a new contact
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py contacts --add "Ambar Singh" "ambar@bayone.com"

# List all saved contacts
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py contacts --list
```

### Check Authentication Status

```bash
python3 {baseDir}/scripts/main.py check-auth --env-file /path/to/.env
```

Reports: auth method, last authenticated, days until refresh token expiry, whether token acquisition works.

## Output Structure

All output at `claude/meeting_transcripts/` from the project root:

```
claude/meeting_transcripts/
├── contacts.json                          # Name-to-email glossary
├── token_cache.json                       # MSAL token cache (gitignored)
├── token_metadata.json                    # Auth timestamps, user email, expiry
├── exports/                               # Exported meeting lists
│   └── 2026-04-14_to_2026-04-16_meetings.csv
├── shalini/                               # Person-based folder
│   ├── 2025-12-09/                        # Date subfolder
│   │   ├── shalini-and-colin-weekly-11_01.txt
│   │   ├── shalini-and-colin-weekly-11_01.vtt
│   │   └── shalini-and-colin-weekly-11_01_meta.json
│   └── 2025-12-16/
│       └── ...
└── cisco-team-sync/                       # Subject-based folder (no person filter)
    └── 2026-04-16/
        └── cisco-team-sync_01.txt
```

## Handling User Requests

When the user asks for transcripts:

1. **Check prerequisites** — Run check_deps.py. Ask for env file path. Run check-auth.
2. **Resolve person** — If user said a name, check contacts.json. If not found, ask for email. Save new contact.
3. **Determine date range** — Parse natural language: "last week", "today", "yesterday", "April 1-15". Default: last 30 days for `--last` queries.
4. **Choose the right command:**
   - "Grab meetings with X" → `fetch --person X`
   - "Find the meeting called Y" → `search --query Y`
   - "What's on my calendar?" → `upcoming`
   - "How many meetings this month?" → `stats`
   - "List everything from last week" → `list --start-date --end-date`
5. **Report results** — Tell user what was found. Present tables/stats in chat.
6. **Handle failures gracefully** — Apply smart timing rules. Report clearly. Ask user what to do next.

## References

- `{baseDir}/references/auth_troubleshooting.md` — Auth flows, common errors, delegated vs application permissions, WSL2 considerations, token lifecycle

## Script Reference

| Script | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `check_deps.py` | Verify pip dependencies are installed |
| `auth_bootstrap.py` | One-time interactive authentication |
| `graph_client.py` | Standalone Graph API client with MSAL token management |
| `calendar_search.py` | Find meetings by person, date, subject. Stats and formatting. |
| `transcript_fetcher.py` | Fetch VTT transcripts from Teams, date-match for recurring meetings |
| `vtt_parser.py` | Parse VTT subtitle format into structured utterances |
| `transcript_formatter.py` | Format parsed transcript to readable text/markdown |
| `contacts.py` | Name-to-email glossary management |
| `main.py` | CLI orchestrator: fetch, search, list, stats, upcoming, contacts, check-auth |

NOTE: Saurav meetings may contain content from other Cisco projects unrelated to CI/CD — those non-CI/CD meetings do not need to be included in this project folder.
